campbell.title: Campbell City Council — July 7, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council reviewed an updated interim ordinance related to state Senate legislation and held public hearings on revoking the business and tobacco permits for two local shops, California Cigar and Hayes Smoke Shop. The council also considered applying for a 'One Big Beautiful' area grant for public works projects and approving new consultant service agreements.
keyTopics: Interim ordinance updating implementation of a state Senate law
Public hearing to revoke business license and tobacco retailer permit for California Cigar
Public hearing to revoke business license and tobacco retailer permit for Hayes Smoke Shop
Public Works Director seeking approval to submit a grant application
Approval of new consultant service agreements

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — June 3, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to decide how much police coverage to fund through its contract with the Santa Clara County Sheriff's Office for the coming fiscal year. Council members reviewed several service level options presented by the Sheriff's Office, each with different staffing and cost implications, and worked toward choosing a near-term approach before deciding on next steps.
keyTopics: Comparing Sheriff's Office service level options for FY 2026
Budget and cost tradeoffs of different patrol staffing levels
Selecting a near-term law enforcement contract approach
Next steps for finalizing the Sheriff services agreement

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 16, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met to approve routine meeting minutes and handle several administrative contract matters. Key business included extending agreements for software services, legal services, and other town contracts, plus adopting a resolution to correct a prior document.
keyTopics: Approval of minutes from the June 2 and June 8, 2026 council meetings
Five-year agreement for software services
Contract amendments for ongoing legal services, including litigation-related legal services
Amendments extending existing town agreements (fifth and second amendments)
Resolution to adopt a corrected version of a prior document

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting covered routine approvals of past council and committee meeting minutes, along with a few business items. Notably, the council reviewed an amendment to a retail concession agreement at the airport and a change to airport fees, plus approval of a City Manager trip to Tampa, Florida.
keyTopics: Approval of City Council meeting minutes
Approval of Council Committee meeting minutes
Amendment to retail merchandising unit concession agreement with PRI-San José, LLC
Amendment to Airport Fees and Charges Rate
City Manager's travel to Tampa, Florida

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to discuss city finances, water planning, and several public honors. Key business included setting next year's budget limits, updating the city's long-term water management plan, and reviewing capital improvement projects like the Public Safety Building and Shoreline Golf Links parking lot repaving. The Council also issued proclamations honoring Immigrant Heritage Month, the Kiwanis Club's 100th anniversary, and City Attorney Jennifer Logue's new judicial appointment.
keyTopics: Closed session on potential litigation exposure (2 anticipated cases)
Proclamations honoring Immigrant Heritage Month, the Kiwanis Club's centennial, and City Attorney Jennifer Logue's judicial appointment
Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget items, including the appropriations limit and financial policy updates
Adoption of the 5-Year Urban Water Management and Water Shortage Contingency Plan
Capital projects update covering the Public Safety Building, Shoreline Golf Links parking lot repaving, and South Bay Salt Pond Restoration

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — July 21, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: Sunnyvale's City Council held a special study session followed by a regular meeting covering routine city business. Key items included a discussion on banning chemical pesticides on city-owned or leased property, approval of routine bill payments and prior meeting minutes, and a contract award for repairs to a water treatment facility.
keyTopics: Proposal to eliminate chemical pesticide use on city-owned or leased property
$1,391,426 contract to Brannon Corporation for levee rehabilitation at the Water Pollution Control Plant's oxidation pond
Amendment to the library's services agreement with Brodart Co. for printed materials and shelf-ready book prep
Routine approval of city bills, claims, and June 16 meeting minutes
Review of upcoming council meeting calendar and board/commission minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 21, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council met in a closed session to discuss three ongoing lawsuits against the City of Cupertino with their legal attorneys. Because this was a closed-door legal strategy session, no public discussion or votes on these cases were part of the open meeting.
keyTopics: Lawsuit: Lixin (Leon) Chen, et al. v. City of Cupertino
Lawsuit: Yes in My Backyard, et al. v. City of Cupertino
Lawsuit: Garden Gate Coalition for Mary Avenue Safety v. City of Cupertino
Closed-session legal strategy discussion with City attorneys

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — July 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: This Santa Clara City Council meeting notice mainly covers how residents can participate, either in person or remotely via Zoom or phone. The posted agenda details focus on meeting logistics and public comment procedures rather than listing specific policy items up for discussion.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and remote participation)
Zoom and phone call-in access details for the meeting
Instructions for submitting written public comment via eComment
Use of the City's Legistar agenda platform for meeting information

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — June 9, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council is reviewing and adopting the city's proposed operating budget for the 2026-27 fiscal year, setting spending priorities for the coming year. The meeting also covers water planning through the 2025 Urban Water Management Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan, plus new rules for backflow prevention (protecting drinking water from contamination) and updates to open governance policies.
keyTopics: Approval of the Fiscal Year 2026-27 city operating budget
Public hearing on the 2025 Urban Water Management Plan
Public hearing on the 2025 Water Shortage Contingency Plan
New ordinance on backflow prevention and cross-connection control to protect water supply
Amendments to municipal code regarding open governance

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — July 8, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council starts with routine business items (director's report, scheduling, and committee assignments) before turning to a public hearing about rezoning a property at 470 Olive Avenue. The proposal would change the site from single-family residential zoning to medium-density mixed-use zoning, aligning it with the neighboring parcel at 2951 El Camino Real, and would update the North Ventura area's land use plan to match.
keyTopics: Rezoning 470 Olive Avenue from single-family residential to medium-density mixed-use
Updating the North Ventura Coordinated Area Plan land use designation
Aligning zoning with the adjacent 2951 El Camino Real parcel
Director's report and upcoming meeting schedule
Council committee assignments
